name: Stray Glitchling
ml_flavor: Noisy data points that wandered off from any known distribution — outliers with legs.
description: A knee-high wobble of mismatched pixels that bumbles through the meadow grass. Mostly harmless, endlessly curious, and prone to charging at anything shiny — including Bit, who does not appreciate it.

name: Meanwing Finch
ml_flavor: Each bird steers toward the average position of its neighbors, showing how a simple mean can organize a whole flock.
description: Round meadow birds that peck between the iris beds in chattering clusters. Startle one and the flock sweeps toward its shared middle before whirling back in a loose, feathery charge.

name: Trail-Loop Leveret
ml_flavor: It memorizes one safe route and repeats it when conditions change, showing overfitting: perfect recall without adaptation.
description: A long-eared meadow runner whose paws have worn the same curling trail through the grass. Small groups race that remembered loop together, even when Kern is standing squarely in it.

name: Patchmunch Bramblehog
ml_flavor: It adjusts its path after each small patch it grazes, like mini-batch learning that updates from a few examples at a time.
description: A burr-coated grazer that nibbles one tiny square of meadow, thinks hard, then trots toward the tastiest nearby patch. When bothered, it snuffles a warning and makes one short, springy lunge.

name: Middleset Mossram
ml_flavor: It keeps moving toward the center of nearby grazers, acting like a cluster centroid that represents the group around it.
description: A broad little ram with mossy curls and iris pollen on its nose. It calmly shuffles to the middle of every grazing herd; scatter its neighbors and it picks a new center before lowering its horns.

name: Rustlewatch Prowler
ml_flavor: It predicts visitors from one feature—rustling grass—and often pounces at wind, showing the weakness of a single-feature classifier.
description: Two moon-bright ears and a soft striped tail are all that show above the tall grass. It waits for one convincing rustle, then bounds out—sometimes at Kern, sometimes at a completely innocent breeze.

name: Lilywise Newt
ml_flavor: It copies the majority color of its three nearest lily pads before firing, showing how nearest neighbors classify by local company.
description: A bright-eyed millpond newt that skips from pad to pad while keeping safely out of reach. Its frill takes the color most common nearby, then flicks a matching bead of harmlessly crackling data.

name: Lastlook Jackdaw
ml_flavor: It copies only the most recent action it observed, a simple form of recency bias that forgets older examples too quickly.
description: A blue-black bird perched on the old ruins, watching every footstep with scholarly intensity. See Kern dodge and it side-hops; swing a sword and it answers with a beak-flicked data bolt.

name: Brackenhoof, Keeper of Forks
ml_flavor: At each pause it asks one simple question—near or far, moving or still—then follows that branch like a decision tree.
description: A stately antlered guardian roaming the meadow's ruined edge, with ferny plates and tiny stone bells. Brackenhoof studies Kern through a sequence of solemn head tilts, chooses a branch, and commits to a grand, clearly signaled charge.
